title: FitDiagnostics output file missing a tree

posts.name: Xingchen Fan
posts.text:
Dear combine experts,
I am using combine v10.1.0. And want to do a bias study. I use GenerateOnly generates 1000 toys. I run FitDiagnosticsover them and want to check the fitting result of them, following the instruction here. First of all, I don’t see atree_fit_sb tree in the output file, and there is no r branch. Secondly, there are 2816 entries in the limit tree which is way more than the number of toys I fit (given some toys fails in the fitting).
Can anyone explain how to access the post-fit signal strength of toys and why there are more entries than my toys in the output file?
Thank you in advance!
Xingchen
posts.cooked_html:
Dear combine experts,

I am using combine v10.1.0. And want to do a bias study. I use GenerateOnly generates 1000 toys. I run FitDiagnosticsover them and want to check the fitting result of them, following the instruction here. First of all, I don’t see atree_fit_sb tree in the output file, and there is no r branch. Secondly, there are 2816 entries in the limit tree which is way more than the number of toys I fit (given some toys fails in the fitting).

Can anyone explain how to access the post-fit signal strength of toys and why there are more entries than my toys in the output file?

Thank you in advance!

Xingchen

posts.name: Nicholas Wardle
posts.text:
Hi,
Are you sure you’re checking the right output file? As in the docs
“In the output file fitDiagnostics.root there is a tree that contains the best fit results under the signal+background hypothesis.” So you need that one, not the usual higgsCombineBlah.root one
Best
Nick

| Xingchen Fan fanxSeptember 9 |

| - |

posts.name: Xingchen Fan
posts.text:
Hi Nicholas,
Yes! Thank you! I was looking at the wrong output file.
I find the tree_fit_sb tree in the correct file. I have one more question. From some printouts, I know that some toys fail the fitting. Does the branch numbadnll record whether a toy is good or not? Otherwise, what is the way to rule out the failing toys?
Best
Xingchen
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Nicholas,

Yes! Thank you! I was looking at the wrong output file.

I find the tree_fit_sb tree in the correct file. I have one more question. From some printouts, I know that some toys fail the fitting. Does the branch numbadnll record whether a toy is good or not? Otherwise, what is the way to rule out the failing toys?

Best

Xingchen

posts.name: Nicholas Wardle
posts.text:
If I remember right there should be a fit status branch in there that you can use - some info on what this means is given here :https://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/latest/part3/nonstandard/?h=fit_st#fit-diagnostics
Best
Nick
